chapters.title: How much more - How much less?
How much do these bottles hold?
Introduction of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Literacy

topics.name: Introduction to various object shapes
topics.summary: Students are introduced to a variety of everyday objects, visually demonstrating different geometric and irregular shapes.

topics.name: Classifying objects by shape
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and categorize objects based on their geometric shapes.

topics.name: Introduction to identifying edges and corners
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and count edges and corners of 3D objects through a game and direct observation.

topics.name: Geometric features of 3D objects
topics.summary: Students practice identifying and counting edges and corners of common three-dimensional shapes.

topics.name: Identifying features of 3D objects
topics.summary: Students identify edges, corners, and faces of 3D objects.

topics.name: Introduction to 3D object nets
topics.summary: Students learn the definition and visual representation of a 3D object's net by examining an opened box.

topics.name: Exploring 3D shapes and their nets
topics.summary: Students engage with activities to understand the faces of a dice and identify nets for an open box.

topics.name: Different views - Different sides
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and differentiate between front, side, and top views of common objects and scenes.

topics.name: Mittu and Meena's journey
topics.summary: Students explore different perspectives, primarily top-down views, by following the journey of two birds over various scenes like a classroom, a village, and a railway station.

topics.name: One box on top of the other
topics.summary: Students practice counting the total number of boxes in various three-dimensional arrangements, enhancing their spatial reasoning.

topics.name: Identifying Shapes in the Classroom
topics.summary: Students observe a classroom illustration and identify objects corresponding to basic geometric shapes like squares, triangles, rectangles, and circles.

topics.name: Understanding Rectangles
topics.summary: Introduction to rectangles, including tracing a soap-box to understand its shape and properties.

topics.name: Understanding Squares
topics.summary: Introduction to squares, including tracing a dice to understand its shape and properties.

topics.name: Identifying and Making Triangles
topics.summary: Activity to identify rectangle and square shaped objects, followed by an introduction to triangles and a paper folding activity to create one.

topics.name: Understanding Circles
topics.summary: Activities to identify triangular objects, distinguish triangles from other shapes, and an introduction to circles through tracing a bangle.

topics.name: Creative Drawing with Basic Shapes
topics.summary: An activity encouraging students to use squares, circles, triangles, and rectangles to draw various pictures.

topics.name: Drawing Shapes on a Dot Grid
topics.summary: An activity where students practice drawing squares, rectangles, and triangles of different sizes by connecting dots on a grid.

topics.name: Rangoli with Shapes and Colours
topics.summary: An activity involving colouring a rangoli design based on specific shapes and colours, followed by counting the shapes and creating a personal rangoli.

topics.name: Chapter Review: Shape Identification and Creation
topics.summary: A set of exercises to review the identification of shapes from objects, counting shapes in complex figures, and creating shapes using clay.

topics.name: Representing numbers with beads
topics.summary: Introduction to representing numbers using beads on a chain and identifying their values.

topics.name: Numbers on a number line
topics.summary: Learning to identify and place numbers on a number line, including numbers between tens.

topics.name: Comparing and ordering numbers in a family context
topics.summary: Using family ages to practice comparing and ordering numbers.

topics.name: Money and number operations
topics.summary: Applying number concepts to money, including comparison, addition, and subtraction.

topics.name: Numbers beyond 100 on a number line
topics.summary: Extending the concept of number lines to represent and locate numbers greater than 100, up to 1000.

topics.name: Writing number names beyond 100
topics.summary: Learning to write the number names for numbers greater than 100.

topics.name: Number names and sequences
topics.summary: Practicing writing number names and completing numerical sequences.

topics.name: Place value and number representation
topics.summary: Understanding place value by representing numbers using currency notes, coins, and beads.

topics.name: Expanded Form and Place Value
topics.summary: Students learn to represent numbers in expanded form using different methods and identify the place value of digits within a number.

topics.name: Forming Numbers from Given Digits
topics.summary: Students learn to create two-digit and three-digit numbers using a given set of digits, including forming all possible combinations.

topics.name: Data Interpretation and Comparison (Cricket Bowlers)
topics.summary: Students learn to read and interpret numerical data presented in a table, compare and order numbers, and perform simple calculations based on the data.

topics.name: Number Concepts: Multiples, Sequences, and Representation
topics.summary: Students explore the composition of large numbers like 1000, identify and complete number patterns, and represent numbers using currency and grouped objects.

topics.name: Comprehensive Number Practice and Problem Solving
topics.summary: This topic provides a comprehensive review and practice of various number concepts, including data interpretation, solving number riddles, writing expanded forms, number names, and forming numbers from digits.

topics.name: Demonstrating addition methods with number line
topics.summary: Students learn different ways to solve addition problems by breaking down numbers and visualizing jumps on a number line.

topics.name: Addition using number decomposition on a beads chain
topics.summary: Students learn to add two-digit numbers by breaking down one number and visualizing steps on a beads chain.

topics.name: Subtraction using beads chain methods
topics.summary: Introduces two distinct strategies for solving subtraction problems visually with a beads chain.

topics.name: Number line strategies for addition and subtraction
topics.summary: Students learn two different methods for performing addition and subtraction using a number line.

topics.name: Applying addition and subtraction to data
topics.summary: Students practice addition and subtraction by interpreting a data table and solving comparison questions, including inverse operations.

topics.name: Introduction to estimation of sums and differences
topics.summary: Students learn to estimate results of addition and subtraction by rounding numbers.

topics.name: Introduction to addition of larger numbers
topics.summary: Students are introduced to adding three-digit numbers by decomposing them into place values.

topics.name: Comparing methods for three-digit addition and subtraction
topics.summary: Students observe and compare different strategies for performing three-digit addition with carrying and subtraction with decomposition.

topics.name: Subtraction with regrouping explanation
topics.summary: Students learn the step-by-step process of subtracting three-digit numbers using regrouping, explained through an example.

topics.name: Introduction to Number Estimation
topics.summary: Students learn to estimate sums and differences by rounding numbers.

topics.name: Exploring Commutative Property of Addition
topics.summary: Students investigate if changing number order in addition affects the sum.

topics.name: Multi-step word problem practice
topics.summary: Students solve various real-world problems involving addition and subtraction.

topics.name: Introduction to Multiplication through Real-World Scenarios
topics.summary: Students are introduced to multiplication concepts through a fruit shop scenario, involving counting, pricing, and calculating total costs for items bought in quantities or by weight.

topics.name: Understanding Multiplication as Repeated Addition/Scaling (Magic Pipes)
topics.summary: Students learn multiplication as a scaling operation (doubling, tripling, multiplying by six, and multiplying by eight) through a story about "magic pipes" that multiply quantities.

topics.name: Strategy for Multiplication: Splitting Numbers (Distributive Property)
topics.summary: Students learn to multiply larger numbers by splitting one of the numbers into smaller, easier-to-multiply parts, demonstrating the distributive property of multiplication.

topics.name: Practice with Multiplication and Word Problems
topics.summary: Students apply multiplication skills to solve various word problems set in different contexts and practice the "splitting numbers" strategy for larger multiplications.

topics.name: Multiplication by Multiples of Ten and Hundred & Pattern Recognition
topics.summary: Students learn to multiply by 10 and 100, and observe patterns in multiplication with multiples of ten, which helps in mental math and understanding place value.

topics.name: Properties of Multiplication and Problem Solving
topics.summary: Students explore the commutative property of multiplication, practice filling in missing factors/products in equations and diagrams, and develop creative problem-solving skills by creating their own word problems from images.

topics.name: Multiplying bigger numbers
topics.summary: This topic introduces methods for multiplying larger numbers, specifically two-digit and three-digit numbers by a single digit, using both expanded form (splitting numbers) and standard column multiplication methods. It includes word problems to apply these concepts.

topics.name: Just estimate!
topics.summary: This topic introduces the concept of estimation in multiplication, specifically for real-world problems involving population and cost. Students learn to round numbers to make quick estimations.

topics.name: Can you estimate closest to the products?
topics.summary: This topic provides practice in estimating products for various real-world scenarios, reinforcing the skill of rounding and quick multiplication.

topics.name: Concept of forming equal groups
topics.summary: Explains how to divide a total quantity into equal groups using a real-world scenario.

topics.name: Understanding division as equal sharing
topics.summary: Students learn to solve division problems by distributing a total quantity equally into a given number of groups, using a real-world scenario.

topics.name: Relationship between division and multiplication
topics.summary: Students learn that division and multiplication are inverse operations and can be used to derive related facts from visual representations.

topics.name: Checking division with multiplication
topics.summary: Students learn to verify division results using inverse multiplication.

topics.name: Applying division to create word problems
topics.summary: Students learn to formulate division word problems based on visual information.

topics.name: Division with remainder concept
topics.summary: Explains the process of division, identifying the quotient and the remainder.

topics.name: Introduction to division with remainder
topics.summary: Students learn division with remainder through examples and step-by-step calculation.

topics.name: Comparing long division methods for larger numbers
topics.summary: Students learn and compare two distinct long division procedures for solving the same problem.

topics.name: Comparing long division strategies
topics.summary: Students observe and analyze three distinct methods for performing long division on the same problem, noting partial quotients.

topics.name: Understanding division through varied methods
topics.summary: Students explore different approaches to solve a division problem and analyze common errors.

topics.name: Do not divide. Just estimate!
topics.summary: Introduces the concept of estimating the quotient of division problems by rounding numbers to easily divisible values, rather than performing exact calculations.

topics.name: Exercise
topics.summary: A comprehensive collection of word problems designed to reinforce understanding and application of multiplication and division concepts in various real-life scenarios.

topics.name: Introduction to Measuring Length
topics.summary: Students are introduced to measuring length using a scale and centimetres (cm), recalling prior knowledge from Class 3. They practice measuring various objects.

topics.name: Estimating and Measuring Body Parts
topics.summary: Students practice estimating and then accurately measuring the lengths of their own body parts using a scale.

topics.name: Measuring Objects and Routes
topics.summary: Students practice estimating and measuring lengths of various classroom objects and apply length comparison to find the shortest path.

topics.name: Measuring Heights and Introducing Metres
topics.summary: Students learn to measure heights using scales with centimetres and inches, and are introduced to the metre as a larger unit of length, along with its conversion to centimetres.

topics.name: Activities with Metres and Centimetres
topics.summary: Students engage in practical activities to understand the relationship between metres and centimetres, estimate lengths in metres, and verify measurements.

topics.name: Choosing Appropriate Units for Measurement
topics.summary: Students learn to decide whether to use metres or centimetres for measuring different objects based on their size.

topics.name: Applying Length Concepts in Real-World Scenarios
topics.summary: Students solve word problems involving lengths and distances in various real-world contexts like sports races and geographical distances, including calculations with metres and centimetres.

topics.name: Problem Solving with Metres and Centimetres
topics.summary: Students solve a variety of word problems involving addition, subtraction, and conversion of lengths expressed in metres and centimetres.

topics.name: Advanced Problem Solving with Length Units
topics.summary: Students apply their understanding of length measurement in metres and centimetres to solve complex word problems, including those involving unit conversion, division, data interpretation from tables, and comparison of lengths in real-world scenarios like sports.

topics.name: Introduction to Weight Measurement
topics.summary: This topic introduces the concept of weight, comparing objects based on their heaviness, and introduces the standard unit of weight, the kilogram (kg).

topics.name: Estimating Weights of Common Objects
topics.summary: Students practice estimating the weight of various common objects, categorizing them as heavier than 1 kg, around 10 kg, or matching them to specific weight ranges.

topics.name: Calculating Total Weight and Cost
topics.summary: Students learn to calculate the total weight of multiple items and apply this calculation to a real-world problem involving cost based on weight.

topics.name: Real-World Weight Problems and Introduction to Grams
topics.summary: This topic presents word problems involving weight calculations for items like cotton, mattresses, and pillows. It also introduces grams as a unit for measuring lighter objects and the conversion factor between kilograms and grams.

topics.name: Estimating and Categorizing Lighter Objects by Weight
topics.summary: Students estimate the weight of very light objects like a pencil and practice identifying items that fit specific weight categories (grams and kilograms) by filling a table.

topics.name: Kilogram-Gram Conversions and Combinations
topics.summary: Students practice converting kilograms to grams and explore different combinations of smaller gram weights that sum up to 1 kilogram, reinforcing the 1 kg = 1000 gm concept.

topics.name: Balancing Scales
topics.summary: Students learn the practical application of weight measurement by balancing weighing scales using a variety of standard weights (kilograms and grams).

topics.name: Advanced Weight Problems and Post Office Rates
topics.summary: This topic challenges students with more complex weight problems, including using a limited set of weights (stones) to measure different amounts and calculating postal charges based on the weight of letters and parcels.

topics.name: Various ways of measuring weight
topics.summary: This topic introduces students to modern electronic weighing devices and provides a practical activity for them to measure and record their own weight and the weights of their friends.

topics.name: Exercise: Weight Problems and Conversions
topics.summary: This topic presents a comprehensive set of exercises covering conceptual understanding of weight, estimation of units, solving word problems involving all four basic arithmetic operations with weights, and converting between kilograms and grams.

topics.name: Comparing capacities to a standard litre
topics.summary: Students learn to compare the capacity of various containers against a 1-litre standard.

topics.name: Estimating container liquid capacity
topics.summary: Students estimate volumes of various containers using given labels.

topics.name: Calculating daily water consumption
topics.summary: Students calculate water needed for daily activities and total consumption.

topics.name: Estimating daily liquid volume in litres
topics.summary: Students practice estimating large liquid quantities in litres for daily activities.

topics.name: Introduction to millilitres and small quantities
topics.summary: Students learn about millilitres as a unit for small liquid volumes and identify items measured in them.

topics.name: Measuring medicine volume using millilitres
topics.summary: Students apply millilitre measurement to read volumes in medical contexts like syringes and medicine caps.

topics.name: Understanding millilitres and litres conversion
topics.summary: Students learn the relationship between millilitres and litres, specifically that 1000 ml equals 1 litre, through a practical demonstration.

topics.name: Introduction to liquid capacity and litres
topics.summary: Students explore liquid measurement, estimating and verifying how different bottle volumes combine to form one litre.

topics.name: Capacity unit conversion and practical application
topics.summary: Students practice converting between liters and milliliters and apply this to a real-world problem of calculating water wastage.

topics.name: Applying capacity and volume concepts
topics.summary: Students solve word problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of liquid volumes, including unit conversions.

topics.name: Calculating total liquid volume from daily scenarios
topics.summary: Students practice calculating total liquid volumes by summing up quantities from various real-life situations.

topics.name: Understanding time duration for activities
topics.summary: Students learn to estimate and compare the time required for various activities, from short to long durations.

topics.name: Time-telling in a story context
topics.summary: Students practice reading analog clocks and calculating time durations within a narrative.

topics.name: Telling time, including minutes and time calculations
topics.summary: Students practice reading time from clock faces, calculate time in the past and future, and learn how the minute hand indicates 5-minute intervals.

topics.name: Introduction to time-telling through a story
topics.summary: Students learn about telling time by following a character's daily schedule.

topics.name: Practice telling time on analog clocks
topics.summary: Students practice reading and setting time on analog clocks.

topics.name: Time and calendar concepts application
topics.summary: Students apply clock knowledge and learn to interpret calendars for festival dates.

topics.name: Full year calendar display
topics.summary: Students observe and understand the structure of a calendar, including months, days, and dates.

topics.name: Calendar interpretation and time concepts
topics.summary: Students learn to read calendars, identify months, days, and understand the concept of a leap year.

topics.name: Calendar and Time-telling Practice
topics.summary: Students practice interpreting a calendar for a given month and creating a calendar for the next month. They also solve word problems involving time duration and tell time from analog clocks.

topics.name: Introduction to equal sharing concept
topics.summary: Students learn the foundational concept of dividing objects into equal groups through practical examples.

topics.name: Introduction to equal division
topics.summary: Students learn division using visual examples and numerical representation.

topics.name: Introduction to dividing into halves
topics.summary: Students learn to divide a whole object into two equal parts, understanding the concept of 'half' and its fractional representation.

topics.name: Concept of dividing into equal parts
topics.summary: Students learn to represent division of whole items into fractions and mixed numbers using examples.

topics.name: Practical division of items among people
topics.summary: Students learn to divide a given number of items equally among a specified number of people, expressing the result in whole and fractional parts.

topics.name: Dividing objects into equal parts and fractions
topics.summary: Students learn to divide whole objects into equal portions and identify the fractional part each share represents, both numerically and visually.

topics.name: Introduction to dividing into equal parts and fractions
topics.summary: Students learn to visually represent and identify fractions by dividing shapes.

topics.name: Practice identifying fractions of a set
topics.summary: Students apply their understanding of fractions to identify and mark a specified portion of a collection of discrete objects.

topics.name: Applying fractions to real-world problems and visual representations
topics.summary: Students apply their understanding of dividing into equal parts and fractions (halves, quarters, etc.) to solve problems involving quantities and identify fractions from visual representations of objects.

topics.name: Observational fraction application and problem creation
topics.summary: Students apply fraction concepts to real-time classroom observations and develop their own fraction-based problems, fostering critical thinking and problem-solving skills.

topics.name: Introduction to Tally Marks and Data Representation
topics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of collecting and representing data using tally marks by analyzing student grades.

topics.name: Which is your favourite game?
topics.summary: Students conduct a survey among their classmates to find out their favourite games, record the data using tally marks, and then analyze the results.

topics.name: How big are the families that live around you?
topics.summary: Students collect data on family sizes in their locality and represent it using tally marks in a table.

topics.name: Types of houses
topics.summary: Students collect data on different types of houses in a village and represent it using tally marks in a table.

topics.name: Picture Tables and Charts
topics.summary: Students learn to interpret data presented in picture tables, specifically focusing on stock levels in a toy shop, and answer questions based on the visual representation.

topics.name: Helping Hands!
topics.summary: Students analyze a picture table showing daily money collection for an orphanage and answer questions about collection trends and reaching a target.

topics.name: Bags of paddy
topics.summary: Students interpret a picture chart (bar chart using images) representing paddy production over several years and answer questions about production trends and comparisons.

topics.name: Box Charts
topics.summary: Students learn to interpret bar charts (referred to as box charts) showing stock levels of different oil types and answer questions involving comparison and simple calculations.

topics.name: Introduction to Perimeter
topics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of perimeter through a real-world example of decorating a blackboard, calculating the total length of material needed for its edges.

topics.name: Fencing the fields
topics.summary: Students apply the concept of perimeter to calculate the length of fencing wire required for various irregular field shapes and determine if a given amount of wire is sufficient.

topics.name: Borders and Laces
topics.summary: Students solve real-world problems involving perimeter calculations for fabric borders (dupatta and sari) and determine the associated costs based on the length of lace required.

topics.name: Exploring Perimeter Concepts
topics.summary: Students investigate how the perimeter of a square changes when parts are removed and are introduced to the concept of measuring perimeter for circular paths, including practical considerations for races.

topics.name: Perimeter Practice Exercise
topics.summary: Students engage in a series of practice problems to reinforce their understanding of perimeter, including calculating perimeters of various complex shapes, comparing perimeters for practical applications like fencing, and determining total distances for multiple rounds around a boundary.

topics.name: Introduction to symmetrical halves
topics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of dividing objects into identical halves using a story and visual examples.

topics.name: Introduction to dividing objects into symmetrical halves
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and draw lines that divide various objects into two identical, mirror-image halves.

topics.name: Identifying symmetrical halves
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and draw lines of symmetry in figures and numbers.

topics.name: Introduction to symmetry through observation and creation
topics.summary: Students learn about objects that can be divided into two identical halves by observing their surroundings and creating a symmetrical mask.

topics.name: Patterns in our surroundings
topics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of patterns by observing repeating elements in everyday objects and natural surroundings, such as boundary walls, grills, leaves, and bridges.

topics.name: Creating and Extending Patterns with Objects and Turns
topics.summary: Students practice recognizing and extending patterns using sequences of beads and by observing arrangements of children that involve changes in orientation or 'turns'. They also get to create their own patterns.

topics.name: Carrying Forward Visual Patterns
topics.summary: Students practice identifying the rule of various visual and geometric patterns and then drawing the subsequent elements to continue the sequence.

topics.name: Patterns in Numbers and Calendar
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and extend patterns in numerical sequences, including increasing and decreasing series. They also explore mathematical patterns found within a calendar grid, such as the sum of numbers in specific blocks.

topics.name: Available AI-Assisted Adaptive Learning tools
topics.summary: This topic introduces students to two types of AI-assisted adaptive learning tools: Assisted Language Learning (ALL) and Assisted Mathematics Learning (AML), outlining their benefits for skill development.

topics.name: 1. Coding for Shapes
topics.summary: This topic introduces basic coding concepts by allowing students to use drag-and-drop blocks to create shapes, demonstrating how code translates into visual output.

topics.name: 2. Chat with a Bot
topics.summary: This topic introduces students to chatbots, their function, and how they can be used to answer questions, particularly math problems, encouraging exploration and critical thinking about AI capabilities.

topics.name: PATTERNS
topics.summary: This topic introduces GCompris as a digital tool to develop logical thinking skills through interactive games, specifically focusing on pattern recognition in the "Lights off" game.

topics.name: TICK TOCK GOES THE CLOCK
topics.summary: This topic introduces eduActive, a free offline educational application, as a tool for children to learn how to set the clock through interactive activities.

topics.name: Maths in Marriage Preparations - Saree Buying
topics.summary: Students learn to apply mathematical concepts like addition, subtraction, and understanding patterns in the context of buying sarees for a marriage.

topics.name: Buying sweets for the marriage
topics.summary: Students apply mathematical operations to calculate the cost of sweets and the number of packages based on weight.

topics.name: Posting wedding cards
topics.summary: Students learn to calculate the cost of postage based on the number of items.

topics.name: Preparation for the journey
topics.summary: Students apply estimation, capacity, distance, and time calculations in the context of planning a journey for a marriage.

topics.name: Marriage party leaves for Warangal / Let's have tea and juice at Karimnagar!
topics.summary: Students apply concepts of time, distance, length, capacity, and cost calculation in the context of a journey and refreshment stop.

topics.name: Purchasing flowers / Try This
topics.summary: Students apply measurement concepts (cubits), time calculation, and identify real-world mathematical applications in daily life.

topics.name: GUIDELINES FOR TEACHER
topics.summary: This section provides pedagogical and curricular guidelines for teachers, outlining the book's structure, learning objectives, teaching methodologies, and specific guidance for teaching shapes and numbers.

topics.name: Syllabus
topics.summary: This topic outlines the mathematics syllabus for the academic year, covering various domains such as spatial understanding, numbers, measurement, data handling, and day-to-day maths, with allocated hours for each section.

topics.name: Academic Standards
topics.summary: This topic details the academic standards expected in mathematics, categorized into problem-solving, reasoning proof, communication, connections, and visualization and representation.

topics.name: LEARNING OUTCOMES
topics.summary: This topic lists the specific learning outcomes for Class 4 Mathematics, outlining what a learner should be able to do by the end of the course.

topics.name: Pedagogical Approaches to Core Math Concepts
topics.summary: This section outlines pedagogical guidelines and objectives for teaching fundamental mathematical concepts to children, focusing on practical approaches, problem-solving, and real-world applications across various domains of mathematics.

topics.name: NOTES
topics.summary: This section provides blank lined pages for students to take notes or practice writing.

topics.name: TEXT BOOK DEVELOPMENT TEAM - 2013
topics.summary: This section lists the various individuals and teams involved in the creation and publication of the textbook, including advisors, editors, writers, illustrators, and the QR code team.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Different Views
subtopics.summary: Introduces the concept that the same object can appear different from various perspectives (front, side, top).

subtopics.name: Identifying Top and Side Views of Objects
subtopics.summary: Students practice identifying top and side views of everyday objects.

subtopics.name: Identifying Front, Back, and Side Views of Locations
subtopics.summary: Students practice identifying front, back, and side views of a house and a car in a scene.

subtopics.name: Classroom from Top View
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a classroom scene from a top-down perspective and answer questions about it.

subtopics.name: Village/School Area from Top View
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a village or school area scene from a top-down perspective and answer questions about it.

subtopics.name: Railway Station from Top View
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a railway station scene from a top-down perspective and answer questions about it.

subtopics.name: Counting Stacked Boxes
subtopics.summary: Students learn to count individual boxes within a stacked arrangement.

subtopics.name: Properties of a Rectangle
subtopics.summary: Discussion and questions about the number of sides and corners of a rectangle.

subtopics.name: Properties of a Square
subtopics.summary: Discussion and questions about the number of sides and corners of a square.

subtopics.name: Properties of a Triangle
subtopics.summary: Discussion and questions about the number of sides and corners of a triangle.

subtopics.name: Properties of a Circle
subtopics.summary: Discussion and questions about the presence of sides or corners in a circle.

subtopics.name: Understanding Expanded Form
subtopics.summary: Students learn to break down a number into its hundreds, tens, and ones components, expressing it as a sum of place values.

subtopics.name: Identifying Place Value
subtopics.summary: Students practice identifying the place value of a specific digit within a given number.

subtopics.name: Forming Two-Digit Numbers
subtopics.summary: Students practice forming different two-digit numbers using two given digits.

subtopics.name: Forming Three-Digit Numbers
subtopics.summary: Students practice forming three-digit numbers using three given digits.

subtopics.name: Generating All Possible Numbers
subtopics.summary: Students learn to systematically generate all possible numbers from a given set of digits.

subtopics.name: Reading Data from a Table
subtopics.summary: Students practice extracting specific numerical information from a structured table.

subtopics.name: Comparing and Ordering Numbers
subtopics.summary: Students compare multiple numbers from a dataset and arrange them in ascending or descending order.

subtopics.name: Simple Calculations with Data
subtopics.summary: Students perform basic arithmetic operations (e.g., subtraction to find a difference or complement) using numbers from the table.

subtopics.name: Understanding Multiples within 1000
subtopics.summary: Students determine how many times smaller numbers (100, 200, 500) fit into 1000.

subtopics.name: Identifying and Completing Number Sequences
subtopics.summary: Students recognize patterns in number sequences and fill in missing numbers.

subtopics.name: Representing Numbers with Currency and Objects
subtopics.summary: Students count the total value of various currency notes, coins, and grouped objects to represent a number.

subtopics.name: Interpreting Data from Tables
subtopics.summary: Students practice extracting, comparing, and ordering numerical information from tables related to real-world scenarios (e.g., cricket scores, animal weights).

subtopics.name: Solving Number Riddles and Word Problems
subtopics.summary: Students apply their understanding of place value, digit properties, and basic arithmetic to solve various number-based riddles and word problems.

subtopics.name: Writing Numbers in Expanded Form
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing three-digit numbers in their expanded form, reinforcing place value understanding.

subtopics.name: Converting Numbers to Word Names
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing the word names for given three-digit numbers and vice versa.

subtopics.name: Forming Largest and Smallest Numbers
subtopics.summary: Students learn to arrange given digits to form the largest and smallest possible three-digit numbers.

subtopics.name: Understanding quantities and prices in a fruit shop
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a fruit shop image with prices per kg and per dozen to understand real-world quantity and cost relationships.

subtopics.name: Doubling (multiplying by 2)
subtopics.summary: Students practice multiplying by 2 using the "magic pipe" concept where items are doubled.

subtopics.name: Tripling (multiplying by 3)
subtopics.summary: Students practice multiplying by 3 using the "magic pipe" concept where items become three times their original quantity.

subtopics.name: Multiplying by six
subtopics.summary: Students practice multiplying by 6 using the "magic pipe" concept where items become six times their original quantity.

subtopics.name: Multiplying by eight
subtopics.summary: Students practice multiplying by 8 using the "magic pipe" concept where items become eight times their original quantity.

subtopics.name: Dialogue introducing the challenge of multiplying numbers beyond basic tables
subtopics.summary: A conversation between two students highlights the need for a strategy to multiply numbers larger than those in basic multiplication tables.

subtopics.name: Explaining and demonstrating splitting a number (e.g., 13 = 10 + 3) to multiply
subtopics.summary: Students are shown how to break down a number (e.g., 13 into 10 and 3) to simplify multiplication, applying the distributive property.

subtopics.name: Demonstrating alternative ways to split a number (e.g., 13 = 8 + 5)
subtopics.summary: The concept of splitting numbers is reinforced by showing that a number can be broken down in multiple ways (e.g., 13 into 8 and 5) to achieve the same multiplication result.

subtopics.name: Applying the splitting numbers strategy to solve multiplication problems
subtopics.summary: Students practice multiplying two-digit numbers by single-digit numbers using the previously learned splitting strategy.

subtopics.name: Solving word problems involving multiplication in different contexts
subtopics.summary: Students solve word problems related to counting objects in rows, passengers in buses, age comparisons, and cost calculations, all requiring multiplication.

subtopics.name: Understanding multiplication by 10 (packets of pencils)
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total number of pencils when given a number of packets, with each packet containing 10 pencils, reinforcing multiplication by 10.

subtopics.name: Understanding multiplication by 100 (boxes of pencils)
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total number of pencils when given a number of boxes, with each box containing 100 pencils, reinforcing multiplication by 100.

subtopics.name: Observing patterns in multiplication by 20, 30, 40, etc.
subtopics.summary: Students complete multiplication tables for multiples of 10 (20, 30, 40) and identify the patterns that emerge.

subtopics.name: Exploring the commutative property of multiplication (a x b = b x a)
subtopics.summary: Students compare the results of multiplying two numbers in different orders to understand that the order of factors does not change the product.

subtopics.name: Filling in missing numbers in multiplication equations
subtopics.summary: Students complete multiplication equations by finding missing factors or products, reinforcing their understanding of multiplication facts.

subtopics.name: Identifying factors and multiples using diagrammatic representations
subtopics.summary: Students analyze and complete diagrams that visually represent multiplication and factorization, showing how numbers can be broken down into their factors.

subtopics.name: Creating word problems based on visual information
subtopics.summary: Students use provided images to formulate their own multiplication word problems, developing their understanding of how multiplication applies to real-world scenarios.

subtopics.name: Multiplication of a 3-digit number by a 1-digit number (325 x 3)
subtopics.summary: Students learn two methods to multiply 325 by 3: Sita's method (splitting 325 into 300, 20, and 5, multiplying each by 3, and then adding the products) and Dharma's method (standard column multiplication with carrying over).

subtopics.name: Multiplication of a 2-digit number by a 2-digit number (45 x 23)
subtopics.summary: Students learn two methods to multiply 45 by 23: Sita's method (splitting 45 into 40 and 5, and 23 into 20 and 3, then multiplying all parts and adding) and Dharma's method (standard column multiplication).

subtopics.name: Estimating population using multiplication
subtopics.summary: Students learn to estimate the total population of a village by multiplying the number of families by an estimated average number of members per family (e.g., 40 families x 4 members = 160 people).

subtopics.name: Estimating cost using multiplication
subtopics.summary: Students learn to estimate the total cost of multiple items by rounding the cost per item to a convenient multiple of ten and then multiplying (e.g., 22 notebooks at ~₹9 each, estimate as 22 x 10 = ₹220).

subtopics.name: Estimation practice with multiple-choice options
subtopics.summary: Students are presented with word problems and multiple-choice options for the estimated product, requiring them to select the closest estimate.

subtopics.name: Estimation in Division
subtopics.summary: Students learn to estimate the result of division problems by identifying numbers close to the dividend that are easily divisible by the divisor, and then selecting the closest estimated answer from given options.

subtopics.name: Solving Word Problems involving Multiplication and Division
subtopics.summary: Students practice reading, understanding, and solving word problems that require the application of multiplication and division operations, including scenarios with remainders and multi-step problems.

subtopics.name: Understanding Centimetres
subtopics.summary: Students learn that centimetres are written as 'cm' in short and how to read a scale marked in centimetres.

subtopics.name: Estimation and Measurement Practice
subtopics.summary: Students learn to first estimate a length and then verify it with actual measurement, applying their understanding of centimetres.

subtopics.name: Comparing Lengths and Paths
subtopics.summary: Students learn to compare different lengths and identify the shortest route among multiple options, reinforcing measurement concepts.

subtopics.name: Centimetres and Inches for Height
subtopics.summary: Students learn about using both centimetres and inches for measuring height and compare the two units.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Metre
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the metre (m) as a larger unit of length, understanding its necessity for measuring longer objects and the conversion: 1 metre = 100 centimetres.

subtopics.name: Metre Strip Activities
subtopics.summary: Students use strips of different lengths (20cm, 25cm, 50cm, 1m) to understand how many smaller units make a metre.

subtopics.name: Estimating and Measuring in Metres
subtopics.summary: Students practice estimating if objects are longer or shorter than a metre and then measure them using a metre rope.

subtopics.name: Metre vs. Centimetre
subtopics.summary: Students practice identifying when to use metres (for longer objects) and when to use centimetres (for shorter objects).

subtopics.name: Sports Day Distances
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a scenario of a 100-metre race and calculate distances between participants.

subtopics.name: Geographical Distances
subtopics.summary: Students calculate distances between houses based on given information, applying addition or subtraction of lengths.

subtopics.name: Javelin Throw Records
subtopics.summary: Students interpret data from a Javelin Throw competition, comparing lengths and calculating differences involving metres and centimetres.

subtopics.name: Length Conversions and Calculations
subtopics.summary: Students practice converting between metres and centimetres and performing arithmetic operations with mixed units of length.

subtopics.name: Solving division problems involving length units
subtopics.summary: Students solve word problems that require converting between metres and centimetres and then performing division to find the number of equal parts.

subtopics.name: Interpreting data tables related to length measurements
subtopics.summary: Students learn to extract and interpret length data presented in a table format, specifically in the context of sports results.

subtopics.name: Calculating differences and comparisons of lengths in real-world contexts
subtopics.summary: Students perform calculations to find the difference between two lengths and compare lengths against given standards (e.g., qualifying limits, world records), using both metres and centimetres.

subtopics.name: Understanding heavier and lighter objects
subtopics.summary: Students learn to compare the weight of different objects and identify which is heavier or lighter through discussion and practical experience.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Kilograms (kg)
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the standard unit of weight, the kilogram, and its abbreviation 'kg'.

subtopics.name: Estimating objects heavier than 1 kg
subtopics.summary: Students identify and encircle objects that they estimate to be heavier than one kilogram.

subtopics.name: Estimating objects around 10 kg
subtopics.summary: Students identify and encircle objects that they estimate to weigh approximately ten kilograms.

subtopics.name: Matching objects to estimated weights
subtopics.summary: Students match images of animals and people to their appropriate estimated weights in kilograms.

subtopics.name: Summing individual weights
subtopics.summary: Students practice adding the weights of various items to find a total weight.

subtopics.name: Calculating cost based on weight
subtopics.summary: Students use the total calculated weight to determine a cost, given a rate per kilogram.

subtopics.name: Solving multi-step weight problems
subtopics.summary: Students solve word problems that require multiple steps of calculation involving weight.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Grams (gm)
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to grams as a unit for measuring lighter objects.

subtopics.name: Understanding 1 kg = 1000 gm
subtopics.summary: Students learn the conversion equivalence between kilograms and grams.

subtopics.name: Estimating weight of a pencil
subtopics.summary: Students consider the approximate weight of a pencil in grams.

subtopics.name: Identifying objects heavier than a pencil
subtopics.summary: Students identify and encircle objects that are heavier than a pencil.

subtopics.name: Categorizing objects by weight
subtopics.summary: Students list various items that fall into different weight categories (e.g., 50 gm, 150 gm, 250 gm, 500 gm, 1 kg).

subtopics.name: Converting 1 kg to 1000 gm
subtopics.summary: Students reinforce the understanding that 1 kilogram is equivalent to 1000 grams.

subtopics.name: Combining different gram weights to make 1 kg
subtopics.summary: Students calculate how many packets of various gram weights (e.g., 500 gm, 100 gm, 250 gm) are needed to make 1 kilogram.

subtopics.name: Practical application of weight measurement
subtopics.summary: Students apply their understanding of weight units to balance a scale visually.

subtopics.name: Using standard weights to achieve balance
subtopics.summary: Students determine which combination of given standard weights (1 kg, 500 gm, 200 gm, 100 gm, 50 gm, 20 gm, 10 gm) is needed to balance a scale.

subtopics.name: Using limited weights to measure different amounts
subtopics.summary: Students solve a problem where they must use a given set of weights (stones) to measure various target weights.

subtopics.name: Calculating postal rates based on weight
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the cost of sending letters and parcels based on a provided rate table that varies by weight.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Electronic Weighing Devices
subtopics.summary: Students learn about various types of electronic devices used for measuring weight, illustrated with images of digital scales.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding of Weight
subtopics.summary: Students engage with a conceptual question to deepen their understanding of weight, distinguishing it from density.

subtopics.name: Weight Estimation and Unit Selection
subtopics.summary: Students practice estimating the appropriate units (grams or kilograms) for various items and identify common objects bought in specific units.

subtopics.name: Solving Word Problems with Weight
subtopics.summary: Students apply their knowledge of weight measurement to solve real-world word problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of weights.

subtopics.name: Kilogram-Gram Conversions
subtopics.summary: Students practice converting weights between kilograms and grams by filling in the blanks in given statements.

subtopics.name: Weight Estimation and Comparison
subtopics.summary: Students estimate the weights of various common objects and then compare their estimates with actual weights, calculating the difference.

subtopics.name: Calendar Interpretation
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions based on a provided calendar for May 2014, focusing on the number of days and specific weekdays.

subtopics.name: Calendar Creation
subtopics.summary: Students are instructed to create a calendar for the month following the one provided (June 2014).

subtopics.name: Time Duration Word Problems
subtopics.summary: Students solve word problems that require calculating time differences and durations of activities.

subtopics.name: Telling Time from Analog Clocks
subtopics.summary: Students identify and write the time shown on analog clocks.

subtopics.name: Dividing geometric shapes into equal parts
subtopics.summary: Students practice drawing lines to divide rectangles into two and four equal parts.

subtopics.name: Solving word problems involving fractions of a quantity
subtopics.summary: Students solve word problems that require calculating a fraction (e.g., 1/2, 1/4, 3/4, 1/5) of a given whole number or quantity.

subtopics.name: Identifying fractions from visual representations
subtopics.summary: Students identify the fractional part of a whole based on visual cues, such as a cupboard with filled/empty shelves or a packet of biscuits with some eaten.

subtopics.name: Applying fractions to classroom observations
subtopics.summary: Students use fraction concepts to describe parts of their classroom environment, such as empty/full blackboards, gender distribution, or attendance, requiring observation and estimation.

subtopics.name: Creating fraction-based problems
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to formulate their own word problems involving fractions based on real-world scenarios, demonstrating a deeper understanding of the concept.

subtopics.name: Tally marks for student grades
subtopics.summary: Students learn to count and record data using tally marks based on a list of student grades.

subtopics.name: Surveying and Data Collection on Favourite Games
subtopics.summary: Students learn to conduct a simple survey, collect data, and represent it in a table using tally marks.

subtopics.name: Family size survey
subtopics.summary: Students learn to conduct a survey to gather information about family sizes and record the data using tally marks.

subtopics.name: House types in a village
subtopics.summary: Students observe and categorize different types of houses, then record the counts using tally marks.

subtopics.name: Abdullah's Toy Shop
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a picture table showing the stock of different toys in Abdullah's shop and answer questions about quantities and preferences.

subtopics.name: Money collected for orphanage
subtopics.summary: Students interpret a picture table representing money collected each day and perform simple comparisons and calculations.

subtopics.name: Malliah's paddy production record
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a picture chart showing paddy production from 2009 to 2012 and answer questions about the highest production, doubling of production, and overall trends.

subtopics.name: Oil merchant's stock record
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a bar chart showing the stock of various oils and answer questions about quantities, comparisons, and sales calculations.

subtopics.name: Understanding perimeter through decorating a blackboard
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify the boundary of a shape and calculate its total length (perimeter) using a practical example of decorating a blackboard.

subtopics.name: Calculating perimeter for fencing
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the perimeter of different irregular polygons representing fields to determine the amount of fencing wire needed.

subtopics.name: Estimating and measuring classroom perimeter
subtopics.summary: Students practice estimating and then accurately measuring the perimeter of their classroom using a measuring tape.

subtopics.name: Calculating lace needed and cost for dupatta
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total length of lace required for a dupatta and its cost based on a given price per meter.

subtopics.name: Calculating lace needed and cost for sari
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total length of lace required for a sari and its cost based on a given price per meter.

subtopics.name: Perimeter of modified squares
subtopics.summary: Students analyze how removing corners from a square affects its overall perimeter, observing changes in length.

subtopics.name: Perimeter of circular paths
subtopics.summary: Students discuss methods for measuring the perimeter of a circular field and consider the implications of different starting positions on a circular racing track.

subtopics.name: Calculating perimeter of complex enclosures
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the perimeter of several irregular and complex shapes with multiple segments.

subtopics.name: Comparing perimeters for fencing needs
subtopics.summary: Students calculate and compare the perimeters of two different fields to determine which one requires more fencing wire.

subtopics.name: Calculating total distance for multiple rounds
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total distance covered by an individual taking multiple rounds around a garden with given dimensions.

subtopics.name: Identifying repeating parts in visual patterns
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify the fundamental repeating unit that forms a larger pattern in various visual examples.

subtopics.name: Extending bead chain patterns
subtopics.summary: Students identify the repeating sequence in bead chains and draw the next elements to extend the pattern.

subtopics.name: Drawing patterns
subtopics.summary: Students create and draw their own patterns, demonstrating an understanding of repeating sequences.

subtopics.name: Identifying patterns with turns
subtopics.summary: Students observe arrangements of people and identify patterns based on their orientation or 'turns'.

subtopics.name: Extending sequences of visual elements
subtopics.summary: Students analyze patterns made of arrows, shapes, and shaded figures, then draw the next items in the series.

subtopics.name: Identifying and extending number series
subtopics.summary: Students analyze given number sequences to determine the rule (addition, subtraction, multiplication) and predict the next numbers in the series.

subtopics.name: Discovering patterns in the calendar
subtopics.summary: Students investigate patterns related to the sum of numbers arranged in specific configurations (e.g., 3x3 blocks, cross shapes) within a calendar month.

subtopics.name: Assisted Language Learning (ALL) tool
subtopics.summary: Explains how ALL tools help improve listening, speaking, word recognition, phoneme/syllable awareness, reading fluency, and conversation skills.

subtopics.name: Assisted Mathematics Learning (AML) tool
subtopics.summary: Explains how AML tools support foundational math skills, generate adaptive tasks based on performance, and boost confidence and fluency in math concepts.

subtopics.name: Let us draw Shapes
subtopics.summary: Students learn that dragging and dropping blocks to make a computer draw shapes is a form of writing code.

subtopics.name: What is Chatbot?
subtopics.summary: Defines a chatbot as a computer program that can understand and respond to questions, providing examples like Alexa, Siri, and Google Assistant.

subtopics.name: LET'S PLAY WITH LIGHTS
subtopics.summary: Explains that GCompris is an application that helps students develop logical thinking skills through interactive games.

subtopics.name: LET'S SET THE CLOCK
subtopics.summary: Introduces eduActive as a free, offline collection of interactive educational activities for primary school children, specifically for learning to set the clock.

subtopics.name: Understanding Saree Patterns and Prices
subtopics.summary: Students observe different patterns on sarees and their associated prices, preparing for cost calculations.

subtopics.name: Calculating Total Cost and Change for Sarees
subtopics.summary: Students practice calculating the total cost of multiple items and determining the change received after payment.

subtopics.name: Calculating Sweet Costs and Packaging
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the total cost of sweets and determine how many boxes are needed for a specific weight.

subtopics.name: Calculating Postage Costs
subtopics.summary: Students use a rate chart to calculate the total cost of posting a given number of wedding cards.

subtopics.name: Estimating Travelers and Bus Capacity
subtopics.summary: Students estimate the number of people traveling and calculate how many people can be seated in a bus and how many buses are required.

subtopics.name: Calculating Travel Costs and Time
subtopics.summary: Students calculate bus fares based on distance and advance payments, and estimate travel time.

subtopics.name: Understanding Travel Time and Distance
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions related to travel time and distance based on a given scenario.

subtopics.name: Measuring Length and Capacity
subtopics.summary: Students engage in dialogue about bridge length and calculate quantities of juice and tea, and their costs.

subtopics.name: Calculating Flower Length and Travel Duration
subtopics.summary: Students calculate the length of flower garlands using cubits and determine travel duration.

subtopics.name: Identifying Mathematical Calculations in Daily Life
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on and identify various mathematical calculations involved in real-life scenarios like marriages and holidays.

subtopics.name: Curricular and Pedagogical Overview
subtopics.summary: Provides an overview of the book's content, including fundamental operations, fractions, number sense, patterns, 2D/3D shapes, spatial understanding, measurement, and data handling, along with pedagogical approaches like real-world examples and collaborative learning.

subtopics.name: General Teaching Strategies
subtopics.summary: Discusses the importance of classroom transaction, Teaching Learning Material (TLM), concept building, problem-solving, and the use of a Hand book for teachers.

subtopics.name: Chapter-wise Guidelines: Shapes
subtopics.summary: Provides specific guidance for teachers on how to teach 2D and 3D shapes, encouraging exploration of objects, understanding features, observing nets, different views, and using local maps.

subtopics.name: Chapter-wise Guidelines: Numbers
subtopics.summary: Provides specific guidance for teachers on how to teach numbers, including the base 10 number system, 100 beads mala, number line, 100 number table, and opportunities for making and comparing numbers.

subtopics.name: I SPATIAL UNDERSTANDING (16 hrs.)
subtopics.summary: Introduction to spatial understanding, including the identification and description of 3D and 2D shapes, their properties, and the creation of pictures and shapes.

subtopics.name: Shapes and Spatial Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify 3D shapes without naming them, identify edges and corners of 3D shapes, distinguish 3D shapes based on roll/slide, identify different views of simple objects, identify nets of cuboid and cube, identify 2D shapes (rectangle, square, triangle, circle), identify 2D shapes in objects, make pictures using known 2D shapes, make shapes on dotted board, understand perimeter, and explore line symmetry.

subtopics.name: Patterns (3 hrs)
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify and carry forward visual patterns.

subtopics.name: II NUMBERS (40 hrs.)
subtopics.summary: Introduction to number concepts up to 1000, including operations like addition and subtraction.

subtopics.name: Numbers upto 1000
subtopics.summary: Students learn to use word problems/contextual situations, read, write, and compare 2, 3-digit numbers, estimate 2, 3-digit numbers on the number line, understand place value in 2, 3-digit numbers, expand numbers using place value, and make numbers using given digits.

subtopics.name: Addition and Subtraction
subtopics.summary: Students learn to use word problems/contextual situations for additions and subtractions up to 999, estimate sums and differences of 2, 3-digit numbers, add and subtract 2-digit numbers on the empty number line, and frame word problems.

subtopics.name: Multiplication
subtopics.summary: Students learn to use word problems/contextual situations for multiplying 1 & 2-digit numbers with 1 & 2-digit numbers using standard (column) algorithm and distributive law, multiply with 10s and 100s, frame word problems, and estimate products.

subtopics.name: Division
subtopics.summary: Students learn to use word problems/contextual situations for dividing 2 & 3-digit numbers with 1 & 2-digit numbers with remainder and without remainder, estimate quotients, frame word problems, and explore the relationship between multiplication and division.

subtopics.name: Fractions
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify half, one fourth and three-fourths of a whole, identify symbols for fractions (1/2, 1/4, 3/4), explain the meaning of fractions, and compare, add, and subtract fractions intuitively.

subtopics.name: Patterns (3 hrs)
subtopics.summary: Students learn to carry forward patterns in numbers based on addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subtopics.name: III Measurement (21 hrs)
subtopics.summary: Introduction to measurement concepts including length, weight, capacity, and time.

subtopics.name: Length
subtopics.summary: Students learn to identify metre and centimetre lengths, relate metre with centimetre, convert metre into centimetre, measure length in metres and inches, estimate length of an object and distance between two given locations, and solve problems involving length and distances in metres and centimetres.

subtopics.name: Weight
subtopics.summary: Students learn to understand weight in terms of kilograms and grams using actual weights and created weights, relate kilogram with gram, estimate the weight of an object and verify it using a balance, solve problems involving weight using kilograms and grams, and appreciate the conservation of weight.

subtopics.name: Capacity
subtopics.summary: Students learn to understand capacity in terms of litre and millilitre, relate litre with ml, measure capacity of given liquid using containers marked with standard units, estimate the capacity of a liquid, verify by measuring, and solve problems involving capacity in litres and millilitres.

subtopics.name: Time
subtopics.summary: Students learn to appreciate the difference in time in terms of minutes, hours, days, and months, read the calendar and identify days, weeks, months in the year, understand leap year, relate date to day on the calendar, read clock time to hours and minutes, and solve problems.

subtopics.name: IV Data Handling (6 hrs)
subtopics.summary: Introduction to data handling, including reading data using pictographs, box charts (bar graphs), and recording data using tally marks.

subtopics.name: Day to day maths (5 hrs)
subtopics.summary: Students learn to solve word problems/contextual situations using more than one operation and/or multiple stages of solving, and estimation in daily life.

subtopics.name: 1. Problem Solving
subtopics.summary: Focuses on using concepts and procedures to solve mathematical problems.

subtopics.name: (a) Kinds of problems
subtopics.summary: Describes various types of problems students will encounter, including forms, puzzles, word problems, pictorial problems, procedural problems, reading data, tables, and graphs.

subtopics.name: Stages of problem solving
subtopics.summary: Outlines the steps involved in problem-solving: reading problems, identifying all pieces of information/data, separating relevant pieces of information, understanding the concept involved, selecting a procedure, and solving the problem.

subtopics.name: (b) Complexity
subtopics.summary: Discusses factors influencing the complexity of a problem.

subtopics.name: The complexity of a problem depends on
subtopics.summary: Factors determining problem complexity: making connections, number of steps, number of operations, amount of context unraveling required, and nature of procedures.

subtopics.name: 2. Reasoning Proof
subtopics.summary: Focuses on reasoning between various steps, understanding and making mathematical generalizations and conjectures, understanding and justifying procedures, examining logical arguments, understanding the notion of proof, using inductive and deductive logic, and testing mathematical conjectures.

subtopics.name: 3. Communication
subtopics.summary: Focuses on writing and reading mathematical expressions, creating mathematical expressions, explaining mathematical ideas in one's own words, explaining mathematical procedures (e.g., adding two-digit numbers with carry over), and explaining mathematical logic.

subtopics.name: 4. Connections
subtopics.summary: Focuses on connecting concepts within a mathematical domain (e.g., multiplication, parts to a whole, ratio, division, patterns, symmetry, measurements, space), making connections with daily life, connecting mathematics to different subjects, connecting concepts of different mathematical domains (e.g., data handling, arithmetic, space), and connecting concepts to multiple procedures.

subtopics.name: 5. Visualization and Representation
subtopics.summary: Focuses on interpreting and reading data in various forms (tables, number lines, pictographs, bar graphs, 2-D figures, 3-D figures, pictures) and making tables, number lines, pictographs, bar graphs, and pictures.

subtopics.name: The learner....
subtopics.summary: An introductory statement for the list of learning outcomes.

subtopics.name: MATHEMATICS CLASS 4
subtopics.summary: Contextual information indicating that these are the learning outcomes for Mathematics in Class 4.

subtopics.name: Reads and writes numerals for numbers greater than 1000 and compares the numbers by using place values of the digits in a number.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to read, write, and compare large numbers using place value concepts.

subtopics.name: Solves simple real life situation problems by using four fundamental operations.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to apply addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve everyday problems.

subtopics.name: Identifies half, one-fourth and three-fourths in a group of objects or an object and represents it in a given picture.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to recognize and represent basic fractions visually.

subtopics.name: Identifies edges, corners and faces of 3D objects and counts them. Prepares net shapes of 3D objects.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to identify and count features of 3D shapes and understand their nets.

subtopics.name: Identifies 2D shapes like triangle, rectangle, square and circle by counting sides and corners.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to identify and describe basic 2D shapes based on their properties.

subtopics.name: Estimates the length or distance in centimeter and meter; weight in gram and kilogram; capacity in liters and milliliters. Verifies by measuring them.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to estimate and measure length, weight, and capacity using standard units.

subtopics.name: Reads the time correctly to the hour and minutes using a clock.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to tell time accurately from a clock.

subtopics.name: Reads the calendar and writes the date in correct format.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to read a calendar and write dates correctly.

subtopics.name: Calculates the perimeter of a geometrical figure formed by line segments.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to calculate the perimeter of simple polygons.

subtopics.name: Extends and creates patterns in simple shapes and numbers.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to recognize, extend, and create simple patterns.

subtopics.name: Represents data in a tabular and pictorial form and draws conclusions.
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to represent and interpret data using tables and pictures.

subtopics.name: Teaching Addition and Subtraction
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching addition and subtraction, including estimation, word problems, and different problem structures ('combine', 'compare', 'change').

subtopics.name: Teaching Multiplication
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching multiplication, covering bigger numbers, various types of word problems (equal groupings, rate, product, array, cartesian product), distributive law, and the relationship with division.

subtopics.name: Teaching Division
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching division, including estimation of quotients and word problems involving grouping and equal sharing.

subtopics.name: Teaching Fractions
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for introducing fractions, understanding fractional numbers, and relating fractions to division and the number line.

subtopics.name: Teaching Measurements
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for introducing concepts of length, weight, time, and capacity, emphasizing practical experiences, various units, and field trips for understanding measurements and conversions.

subtopics.name: Teaching Patterns
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching children to identify, create, and make number patterns.

subtopics.name: Teaching Symmetry
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching symmetry, including identifying symmetrical objects, observing symmetry in nature, and using mirrors for line symmetry.

subtopics.name: Teaching Data Handling
subtopics.summary: Guidelines for teaching data handling, focusing on collecting and organizing data using tables, pictographs, and bar charts.

subtopics.name: Blank Notes Pages
subtopics.summary: Two consecutive pages with horizontal lines provided for students to write notes or practice.

subtopics.name: Chief Advisors
subtopics.summary: Lists the Chief Advisors for the textbook development.

subtopics.name: Editors
subtopics.summary: Lists the Editors for the textbook development.

subtopics.name: Writers
subtopics.summary: Lists the Writers for the textbook development.

subtopics.name: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Digital Literacy Writers
subtopics.summary: Lists the writers specifically for the Artificial Intelligence and Digital Literacy content.

subtopics.name: Academic Support Group
subtopics.summary: Lists the members of the Academic Support Group.

subtopics.name: Illustrators
subtopics.summary: Lists the Illustrators for the textbook.

subtopics.name: Text Book Publication Team
subtopics.summary: Lists the members of the Text Book Publication Team.

subtopics.name: QR CODE TEAM
subtopics.summary: Identifies the team responsible for QR code integration in the textbook.

exercises.text: Categorize objects by shape into a table and add new examples.
Discuss how different object shapes roll or slide.
